name: AWS WAF — Bot Control Managed Rule Group Baseline
objective: Add AWS Bot Control in Count mode to profile automation pressure on login, search, and public application paths before enforcing stronger bot mitigation.
risk_mitigated: Commodity automation, scraping, low-and-slow reconnaissance, and bot-led abuse against browser-facing application paths.
potential_side_effects: Bot Control adds cost and can flag legitimate automation, SEO crawlers, and partner integrations if tuned too aggressively.
tuning_notes: Start in Count mode and review labels, verified bot behavior, and top categories before enabling challenge or block controls. Exclude partner and crawler workflows only after validation.
deployment_notes: Prefer this pack for browser-facing applications where automation pressure is material. Pair with dedicated API controls for machine-to-machine endpoints instead of reusing one bot posture everywhere.
monitoring_notes: Review CloudWatch metrics, labels, and sampled requests for verified bots, likely automation, and repeated login pressure. Coordinate with business teams before blocking high-value crawlers or integrations.
